author: Harrison B.
msg:
How many UNO’s have to speak up about Team communication issues for there to be a change? 

I want to be very specific: 

Communication of “great announcements” & “great progress” is very different from communicating known errors and timelines for a fix.

If we were back in the process of buying Nodes do we think in the current position with the knowledge, or lack of knowledge we know you would invest $5,000 per node?

author: Harrison B.
msg:
And actual team member should read a message like the one above ⬆️ and say,

“Our fellow UNO’s, we understand your frustration, and I’m working tirelessly to get a fixed for you as soon as possible. We understand it is not sustainable for you to keep your ISPs online, paying every month when you’re not making any rewards. We expect to have a fixed in one week, and if we do not have a complete fix by then, we will communicate one week from today on the next steps for our UNO’s”

author: Harrison B.
msg:
New tasks are not the issue. We went from a software verification check to a hardware verification check.

Now, no matter how much money we invested in the hardware, we either have to adapt by adding components to each hardware device, or just buy a whole new device to run the Unity App to not get the current attestation errors..

author: OLLSTAR
msg: Calibration should be running 24/7 365 anyway.

author: Calus B
msg:
Well the delay has been answered sort of. One of the devs had a situation happen out of their control. This is something that they have told us they will not likely release for privacy reasons. The other thing they said is they should have had contingencies in place for that. They mention  that they were a bit too relaxed and it should not have happened. 

So that is the answer we got and I am not sure we will get any more then that. 

Unity nodes sold we have not heard either. I am not sure why this information has been held back. I will ask in the next call

author: Adam Buracchi
msg:
#amaquestion 
#ama

Is the team aware of how disconnected things feel from the community’s perspective?

You continue to say that everything is progressing well, but from where we stand, that doesn’t reflect reality:

1) Most Q1 roadmap items have not been met
2) The CLI is still non-existent, and many ULOs/UNOs purchased SIMs specifically for this purpose
3) SMS tasks non existent 
4) R1 is still active — can you clarify why?
5) New tasks have been coming soon in 7–10 days since January! 
6) It’s unclear whether there are active clients at this stage

At the moment, communication feels very limited, and the community is largely in the dark about what’s actually happening.

Many of us have invested significant time and money into growing the edge network. At the very least, we’re asking for transparency and an honest update on the current situation.

author: Unity Network Assistant
msg:
More CLI and SMS volume is scheduled to begin on Monday. There are already ULOs actively receiving CLI and SMS related tasks on the network, and have been since release.

As we’ve said - volume scales as customers move into production usage, and that ramp-up is now happening.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

author: Unity Network Assistant
msg:
No offense taken - but let’s look at the facts. Tasks have been released. Telemetry tasks have improved and will continue to improve. Voice CLI testing is live. SMS CLI and content testing is live. Larger volumes from customers are expected starting Monday. Scout and Runner Alpha has also been producing rewards and is due for public release. 

Multiple additional tasks are due to launch on the network. That’s not the same story since January - that’s delivery.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ Let’s keep things in perspective.

It has been asked a couple of times that could someone post a screenshot of incentives received from those, but no-one has. So again with all the respect, I think many people in here tend to believe the CLI and SMS tasks aren’t real or working yet. People are worried.

author: MikiRoma87 Miki
msg:
nobody insinuates anything, you are simply making a network statistic based on 3 iphones, and even if it was 30, unless it's 30 on 30 rather than 30 on 100, does not make sense. 
You want to make a point like a person that hits his head on the car roof while entering the car and states "car roofs are useless and are made to hurt your head"  assumptions and points of view are not "reality based", but again rather than complaining here and saying it's useless to report it hence you didn't, bring your 2 cents by reporting it. 
I have reported certain things on the management page that was just released and pleased to see that some have already been implemented. 
If nobody reports: nothing improves. 

It's the basic of any security and safety-based company with many employees and public services.

Internal reports need to have a volume, a team will assess and assign the priorities. 

Things don't happen overnight, sometimes not in a year. 

Complaining here over and over the same things for months, may not be tiring for you, but gets tiring for everyone reading you and then results like this post as a 'last drop'.

Going back to the topic. a statement on the network based on 3 iphones is not a real 'reality based' statistic but rather an assumption based on your own experience. Thanks for sharing but useless to read. 

I hope you get my point, I tried getting yours and I hope if you're so unhappy that you will manage to sell your nodes or learn to report things properly rather than keep ranting here.

My2Cents.

I have 4 phones with pay as you go sims all 100% UNO.

author: Ricky
msg:
Go on default filter (default for leased , low to high for uptime), select “all” license, then deselect the ones u want to keep as leased and bound, and click for revoke for all 
It would show u how many are being revoked (as in leased but not taken by any device)

I recommend doing it as 50 or 100 per page



I did it that way

author: Ricky
msg: One more step before that

author: Ricky
msg:
Make a default setting for all licenses through bulk setting and make it visible
Else u will revoke the license but it would stay hidden

author: Ricky
msg:
Now these are ways around…. Which I learnt the hard way

And , it’s not a bug…. But team can’t improve upon it soon.
As they are busy recalibrating the whole system

Well, we alread, know that it can be successful, i.e  that it can generate more money than the mentioned $96. So that point has been proven. And that was the whole reason for my post. 

Having $96 as a "conservative" number doesnt mean that everyone **will** reach it. But that lots of people **could** reach it.

On S&R you can do 32 calls a day as a scout. Each call gives you 0.1 UPs (assuming you dont find a route). So that makes 3.2 UPs a day and with a month having around 30 days on average, thats 96 UPs per month 😃

msg: Not a day. ~Every 48 hrs

author: Willian | Nuvola
msg:
Or if there's a meaningful number of verified ranges across all countries so runners can jump in and contribute. If some countries don't receive enough verified ranges, we can possibly see a concentration of UNOs filtering some regions out. Whenever that option comes. 

UN needs paying tasks (real rewards coming) in order to make sense for some people to really go out and find quality ULOs.
